content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662         322-5660        322-5901       322-6441        322-6444


January 31, 1985


Michael R. Wave
1220 Howard Avenue, Suite 250
P.O. Box 208
Burlingame, CA  94011

                                       Re: Your Request for Advice
                                           Our File No. A-85-009

Dear Mr. Nave:

     Thank you for your letter requesting advice on behalf of a
member of the City Council for the City of Dublin regarding her
duties under the conflict of interest provisions of the
Political Reform Act.1/

                              QUESTION

     Must a member of the City Council disqualify herself from
participating in decisions concerning the dissolution of the
Community Services District for the City because her spouse is a
director of the Community Services District and receives a
salary for his services as director?

                             CONCLUSION

     The member of the City Council is not required to
disqualify herself from participating in decisions concerning
the dissolution of the Community Services District because her
spouse is a director of the Community Services District and
receives a salary for his services as a director.

____________________
1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise
indicated.

Michael R. Nave
January 31, 1985
Page 2


DISCUSSION

The Political Reform Act prohibits a public official from
making, participating in, or using his official position to
influence a governmental decision in which the official has a
financial interest. Section 87100. Pursuant to Section 87103,
a public official has a financial interest in a governmental
decision when it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision
will have a material financial effect, distinguishable from its
effect on the public generally, on, among other interests, any
source of income aggregating $250 or more received by or
promised to the public official within 12 months prior to the
time when the decision is made.

Income includes any community property interest in the
income of a spouse. Section 82030(a). However, income does not
include any salary and reimbursement for expenses or per diem
received from a state, local, or federal government agency.
Section 82030(b)(2). Therefore, the income to the
Councilmember's spouse from the Community Services District is
not considered income for purposes of the Political Reform Act,
and the Community Services District is not a source of income to
the Councilmember.

Accordingly, the member of the City Council is not required
to disqualify herself from decisions concerning the dissolution
of the Community Services District because of the income her
spouse receives as a director of the Community Services
District.

If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Very truly yours,

Kathryn E. Donovan
Counsel
Legal Division

KED:plh

MICHAEL R. NAVE
ATTORNEY AT LAW
1220, HOWARD AVENUE, SUITE 250

JAN 16 1985 BURLINGAME, CALIFORNIA MAILING ADDRESS
94010-4211 P. O. BOX 208
(415) 348-7130 BURLINGAME, CA 94011-0208


January 14, 1985


Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, California 95814

Re: Request for Advice Letter

Gentlemen:

The City of Dublin respectfully requests a conflict of
interest advice letter. The pertinent facts are as follows:

The City of Dublin incorporated in 1982. Since a date
long prior to incorporation, the sewer, fire prevention and
recreation services for the area now within the City have
been provided by a Community Services District. The City may
be considering the initiation of proceedings to dissolve the
Community Services District. A council member's spouse is an
elected Director of the Community Services District. The
spouse receives a monthly compensation for serving as a Director
but the compensation is not the spouse's major source of income.

Does the Dublin council member have a conflict of interest
that disqualifies her from participating in council discussions
and voting on the dissolution of the Community Services District?

Please send the Advice Letter to the undersigned City
Attorney.

Thank you.

Very truly yours,

[signature]

MICHAEL R. NAVE
City Attorney
City of Dublin

MRN/lm

cc: Richard Ambrose

the dissolution of the Community Services District because her 
spouse is a director of the Community Services District and 
receives a salary for his services as a director. 
1Michael R. Nave 
January 31, 1985

Section 87100. 
Pursuant to Section 87103, 
a public official has a financial interest in a governmental

Section 82030(a). 
However, income does not 
include any salary and reimbursement for expenses or per diem

District.
